messages.content: Tell me a random fun fact about the Roman Empire

Ancient Romans used **urine** to wash their clothes and **whiten their teeth**.

Urine contains ammonia, a powerful bleaching agent. Because of this, it was a highly valued commodity in the empire.

It was so valuable, in fact, that Emperor Vespasian placed a tax on the urine collected from public restrooms. When his son Titus complained that taxing urine was disgusting, Vespasian held up a gold coin collected from the tax and famously said, *"Pecunia non olet,"* which translates to: **"Money does not stink."**
